title: Watch: Rahul Gandhi dragged away, forcibly removed from protest site; pics show nosebleed
content: Congress released videos showing dramatic scenes of senior leaders' detention. Rahul Gandhi was seen lying on the road during the protest. Priyanka Gandhi Vadra resisted boarding a police bus as she was pulled inside. Police forcibly removed and detained several opposition leaders outside the Prime Minister's residence. The protest occurred over the NEET issue and alleged police action on students.

title: No apology: Argentina's Paredes breaks silence after World Cup final chaos vs Spain
content: Leandro Paredes addressed the World Cup final chaos without apology. FIFA has initiated a disciplinary investigation into the post-match incidents. Television footage showed Paredes involved in altercations with Spanish players. Argentina's team also faced criticism for their actions after the match. The investigation could lead to retrospective sanctions for those involved.

title: 'E20 petrol may cut mileage by up to 5%, but no damage to engines': Govt tells Parliament
content: In a written reply in the Rajya Sabha, Minister of State for Petroleum and Natural Gas Suresh Gopi said fuel economy is influenced by several factors, including driving style, vehicle maintenance, tyre pressure, wheel alignment and the use of air conditioning. The government said the drop in mileage for some older vehicles is generally limited to around 3 to 5 per cent.

title: Intel to cut jobs in Data Center and AI Group: What the chipmaker said
content: Intel is reportedly embarking on another round of layoffs within its data center group as part of a strategy to enhance efficiency and agility through better organizational alignment. Despite these job cuts, product commitments and roadmaps are expected to remain intact. Over the last four years, Intel's workforce has seen a significant decline, and the chipmaker is set to unveil its quarterly financial results this week.

title: 'Conditions were challenging': Tilak Varma reveals what went wrong for India
content: Tilak Varma discussed India's recent T20I series losses against Ireland and England. He cited adapting to overseas conditions as a significant challenge for the team. Varma emphasized the importance of staying calm and fostering a positive team environment. The team has learned valuable lessons from these difficult overseas campaigns. India aims to regain momentum by executing these learnings in upcoming matches.

title: 'Where are our leaders being taken?': Cong demands Rahul, Priyanka's whereabouts after detention
content: Congress leaders marched to Prime Minister's residence demanding his and the Education Minister's resignation.  They protested brutalities against students and demanded accountability for examination irregularities.  Police detained several senior Congress leaders during the demonstration in New Delhi.  Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi met injured students admitted at Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital.  The party stated students' demands are clear regarding paper leaks and ministerial responsibility.

title: From eligibility to taxes: What NRIs need to know before buying a home in India
content: Non-Resident Indians can now easily buy residential property in India.  Payments must flow through recognized banking channels, and cash deals are prohibited.  Banks offer loans to NRIs on terms similar to resident buyers.  The PMAY subsidy scheme is not directly available to NRIs.  Affordable housing options are emerging in various Indian cities.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: Amazon river dolphin: The pink mammal behind centuries of myths
content: The Amazon river dolphin, known as the boto, is a unique freshwater mammal. This species is famous for its striking pink color and remarkable intelligence. It has adapted to life in murky rivers, relying on echolocation for navigation. Indigenous folklore portrays the boto as a magical being and river protector. Habitat degradation and human activities now threaten its survival.

title: Messi’s 70-ton monument brings fans to tiny Patagonian village
content: A colossal 26-meter statue of Lionel Messi was recently inaugurated in Cutral Co, Patagonia, showcasing the legendary captain in a kneeling pose with his 2022 World Cup trophy. Another homage, a mural in Berazategui, features the names of more than 1,300 fans, illustrating Messi's profound impact. These monumental creations not only commemorate Messi's successes but also unite the Argentine community and draw tourists from afar.

title: China to country’s AI startups and chipmakers: Do not let tech fall into Western hands
content: Chinese regulators are reportedly considering stricter export controls on AI technology.  This move follows the cancellation of a significant acquisition deal by Beijing.  The government aims to prevent Western firms from acquiring advanced Chinese AI capabilities.  They also seek to restrict overseas manufacturing of proprietary advanced semiconductor designs.  However, tech leaders warn that over-regulation could hinder China's AI development.

title: A look at Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million-dollar property portfolio
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: 'Ghor paap': PM Modi on NEET paper leak; vows strict action against culprits
content: More than 170 people, including CJP protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said. According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC).

title: Justin Langer frontrunner for England coach job; Shastri, Dravid not in the race
content: Former Australia coach Justin Langer has emerged as the frontrunner to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test coach. While the ECB hopes to make an appointment before next month's Pakistan series, former India coaches Ravi Shastri and Rahul Dravid are not part of the recruitment process.

title: 'Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai' to return on OTT; R Madhavan to host
content: The beloved talk show 'Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai' is making a comeback on Zee5, reimagined as 'Jeena Isi Ka Naam Hai 2.0' with R. Madhavan as the host. Madhavan emphasizes the show's ability to forge authentic human connections that transcend the digital sphere. This new season will spotlight remarkable individuals and trendsetters who have made their mark on the world stage.

title: Hallie Aiono’s heartbreaking confession revives Ryan Clark controversy
content: Ryan Clark's past remarks about Puka Nacua's ex, Hallie Aiono, have resurfaced after his reported ESPN exit. Hallie revealed the analyst's public accusations of infidelity during her pregnancy left her emotionally devastated, saying she "did not want to live anymore." She denied cheating on Puka and described the podcast comments as one of the darkest experiences of her life.

title: Neymar’s inspiring journey: From poverty to football greatness
content: Neymar Jr is one of football’s biggest stars today, but his journey began in very difficult circumstances. A heartbreaking moment from his childhood became the reason he never stopped believing in himself. Years later, he turned that dream into reality. His story is a powerful reminder that even the toughest beginnings can lead to something extraordinary.

title: Another oil shock loading for India? How Red Sea disruptions may hit Russian crude supply
content: Iran-backed Yemeni group, the Houthis, have threatened a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. What this essentially means is that Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports are set to be hit. And India which gets a big portion of its crude imports from the Middle East country.

title: 'Criticising him isn't justified': Sikandar Raza defends Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi can become a generational talent. He urged patience for the 15-year-old after his challenging debut series. Raza highlighted the importance of proper management for the young cricketer's development. Zimbabwe anticipates an entertaining T20I series against India. Both teams are in transition, making the contest more evenly balanced.

title: Turkey's main opposition splits as Ozgur Ozel launches new party
content: Turkey's opposition leader Ozgur Ozel announced his departure from the Republican People's Party. He and his allies are forming a new political party after a court ruling. This move follows a court decision that reinstated former leader Kemal Kilicdaroglu. Ozel stated the new party marks a hopeful new beginning for opposition politics. The split could potentially strengthen President Erdogan's long-standing grip on power.

title: 'My first target will be to wear the India jersey': Ashok Sharma eyes dream debut
content: Fast bowler Ashok Sharma received his maiden senior national team call-up. He hopes to make his international debut during the upcoming T20I series. Sharma aims to take wickets and play as many matches as possible. He also dreams of representing India in Test cricket soon. Dale Steyn remains his biggest inspiration for fast bowling.

title: Tracking threats before they reach border: India's next line of defence taking shape in space
content: India is deploying a 52-satellite constellation for enhanced surveillance. This program aims to bolster national security and maritime domain awareness. Private firms will build 31 satellites, while ISRO constructs the remaining twenty-one. The constellation will provide persistent intelligence and secure communications across vast areas. This initiative strengthens India's defense capabilities against evolving threats.

title: How constant niggles and breakdowns are hampering 2027 ODI World Cup plans
content: India's injury list is growing: Nitish Kumar Reddy injured himself during the ODI series against Afghanistan last month, Hardik Pandya has yet to feature in an ODI for India since the Champions Trophy final against New Zealand in March 2025. Harshit Rana has suffered two injury setbacks in the last six months. Washington Sundar has had a couple of breakdowns in the same period. Varun Chakravarthy suffered a hairline fracture before a Grade 2 hamstring injury ruled him out of action.

title: The future of flying? US speeds up plans for air taxis, supersonic planes and drones
content: The FAA is updating aviation rules to encourage new technologies.  It supports air taxis and supersonic passenger flights with new regulations.  Pilot programs are preparing for the safe integration of electric air taxis.  Companies are developing charging infrastructure for electric aircraft operations.  The agency also aims to expand drone use and commercial space launches.

title: 'When I needed someone, he was there': PV Sindhu recalls Kohli's role in comeback
content: PV Sindhu received crucial guidance from Virat Kohli during a difficult career period.  This conversation helped her overcome a serious injury and focus on rehabilitation.  Her husband facilitated her recovery away from public scrutiny in Atlanta.  Sindhu then achieved a historic Japan Open women's singles title win.  This victory marked a remarkable comeback after her extensive recovery process.

title: Qatar Airways denied boarding to infant; court awards family Rs 10 lakh
content: A Kerala consumer commission ordered Qatar Airways to pay ten lakh rupees compensation.  The airline denied boarding to two children, including an infant, despite issuing boarding passes.  This action was deemed a deficiency in service and unfair trade practice by the commission.  The family suffered significant mental agony and hardship due to the airline's arbitrary decision.  Qatar Airways must comply with the order within forty-five days.

title: Can your landlord install CCTV camera outside your rented home? Here's what law says
content: Landlords cannot assume unrestricted rights to monitor tenants' private spaces.  Surveillance invading a tenant's private area may violate their privacy rights.  The Supreme Court affirmed privacy as an intrinsic part of personal liberty.  A Calcutta High Court ruling supported tenants' privacy against intrusive cameras.  Laws protect tenants from intentional capture of private areas without consent.

title: Enzo Fernandez reacts after red card in Argentina's World Cup final loss to Spain
content: Enzo Fernandez expressed pride in representing Argentina after their World Cup final defeat.  He highlighted the team's commitment and effort over the final result.  The midfielder thanked fans for their unwavering support throughout the tournament.  He stated that wearing his country's shirt is the greatest honor.  Fernandez pledged to continue giving his all for Argentina.

title: Delhi HC orders immediate transfer of Sonam Wangchuk to Medanta Hospital
content: Sonam Wangchuk received Delhi High Court permission to move hospitals. He is on a hunger strike for the alleged NEET paper leak. The court ordered his transfer to Medanta Hospital for continued treatment. Safdarjung Hospital will provide all necessary medical treatment details. A panel of doctors will oversee his care at the new facility.

title: China conducts rare live-fire drill inside Japan's claimed EEZ
content: China conducted a live-fire naval drill within Japan's claimed exclusive economic zone. Tokyo lodged a diplomatic protest, citing potential navigation dangers and safety concerns. Beijing dismissed Japan's protest, disputing the legal basis for the EEZ claim. This exercise is viewed as part of growing military coordination between China and Russia. The incident revives a long-standing dispute over Okinotorishima's status.

title: One of America's oldest homes is for sale and it comes with a $1.25 million price tag
content: A rare 1678 William Haskell House is for sale in Gloucester, Massachusetts. This historic colonial-era property offers a glimpse into early American architecture. The nearly 350-year-old home retains original features and modern amenities. It was built by William Haskell, who harvested salt hay from local marshes. The estate now includes an antique barn and a covered porch.

title: ‘Won’t give another retake’: Shekhar Suman recalls working with rekha in ‘utsav’:
content: Shekhar Suman fondly recalled his Bollywood debut opposite Rekha in Utsav. He shared how Rekha made him feel comfortable during intimate scenes. Suman also reminisced about working with Madhuri Dixit in his second film. He accepted supporting roles and played a negative character in Naache Mayuri. Audiences disliking his role in Pati Parmeshwar was a compliment.

title: 'Turned his car around and drove away': Mamata's swipe at PM Modi over CJP Parliament march
content: Mamata Banerjee criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for leaving Parliament during a protest.  A satirical group called the Cockroach Janta Party marched towards Parliament yesterday.  Protesters clashed with police, resulting in injuries on both sides.  The CJP demands the education minister's resignation over various issues.  Banerjee stated she would join the CJP protest if necessary.

title: CJP's 'Sansad Chalo': Young woman injured in police action on ventilator
content: A young woman is on ventilator support after sustaining injuries during a protest. Thousands of CJP supporters marched towards Parliament demanding the education minister's resignation. Police used lathis and tear gas to disperse protesters who tried to breach barricades. CJP accused Delhi Police of excessive force and brutality against peaceful demonstrators. The protest entered its second day with leaders continuing their sit-in and hunger strike.

title: India Test squad for Lanka tour likely this week; selectors sweat over Sundar
content: India's selectors are set to pick the squad for the two-Test tour of Sri Lanka, with Washington Sundar's hamstring injury the biggest selection concern. Jasprit Bumrah and Ravindra Jadeja are expected to return, while decisions on Sundar's replacement and the all-rounder balance remain pending. India need a strong series to boost their World Test Championship qualification hopes.

title: MP high court seeks state’s reply on 'teacherless' government schools
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court has issued notice to the state government regarding schools. Nearly 1,900 government schools are reportedly running without any teachers assigned. An audit revealed 1,895 schools had no teachers, including those with enrolled students. Teachers are also unevenly posted, with some schools having excess staff. The court seeks to address this violation of children's right to education.

title: EXPLAINED: Why Spain players cut the goal nets after winning World Cup
content: Spain celebrated their 2026 FIFA World Cup victory by cutting down goal nets.  This tradition was borrowed from American basketball championship celebrations.  Ferran Torres scored the winning goal in extra time against Argentina.  Spain secured their second World Cup title after a dominant performance.  The players took pieces of the net as souvenirs of their triumph.

title: ‘We are eating leaves’: Assam floods make boy spend night on tree, others on rooftops
content: Torrential runoff from Nagaland triggered devastating floods across Assam's Sivasagar and Charaideo districts. Thousands of families were marooned, with many taking refuge on rooftops and trees. A viral video highlighted the plight of a boy and his family surviving on leaves. Former MLA Debabrata Saikia described the situation as terrifying and critical. Many stranded individuals faced challenges reaching authorities due to communication failures.

title: Six months of UDF rule in Kerala’s Pala municipality ends with ouster of chairperson Diya Binu
content: No-confidence motion, moved by LDF against Diya Binu, regarded as the youngest chairperson of any civic body in the country, passed with support of 17 councillors, including four Congress members and UDF vice-chairperson

title: Kumily panchayat plans to set up CSTP plant to prevent wastewater from flowing into Periyar lake
content: Without a proper treatment system, untreated wastewater from tourism establishments flows into a canal that feeds directly into the Periyar lake within the Mullaperiyar reservoir. Local body officials discovered that several businesses were discharging wastewater directly into culverts connected to the canal

title: GoM to examine phased shift of bulk cargo from Visakhapatnam Port to Mulapeta Port
content: Proposal aims to reduce pollution, ease congestion at Visakhapatnam Port and enable commercially viable operations at Mulapeta Port, says Minister

title: Shivamogga Deputy Commissioner holds meeting on forming Bharat Jodo Yuva Sanghas
content: Shivamogga Deputy Commissioner Prabhulinga Kavalikatti tells officers that the State government had planned to form 10,000 Bharat Jodo Yuvaka Sanghas in the State.

title: Toxic gas kills 12 in Sikkim hydropower tunnel
content: NHPC says 25 workers were inside the under-construction tunnel of the Teesta State-VI Hydroelectric Project in Namchi district and efforts are underway to rescue those still trapped inside

title: AAP takes dig at Congress' dharna outside PM's residence, says Rahul 'undermining' CJP protest
content: Delhi AAP chief Saurabh Bharadwaj also attacked the Congress, alleging that its leaders were silent on the ongoing movement, while their social media handles and influencers aligned to them have been trying to “weaken” the CJP protest

title: Kerala HC extends stay on coercive action against former DGP
content: Ms. Sreelekha had allegedly divulged details that could identify minor rape survivors from the Kiliroor and Kaviyoor sexual assault cases through her YouTube Channel

title: GBA engineer assaulted during footpath clearance drive near Shivajinagar, 10 persons arrested
content: Authorities said all those involved in the attack will be booked under Section 109 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, which deals with attempt to murder

title: APSSDC launches campaign to boost participation in IndiaSkills Competition 2026-27
content: IndiaSkills Competition is India’s premier skills championship, designed to identify and nurture exceptional talent across a wide range of 63 vocational and technical trades

title: Rahul, Priyanka, Akhilesh detained from protest site near PM's residence; thousands regroup at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest site | LIVE
content: Govt wasted our time, says Dipke on talks between Nadda, CJP team; Delhi High Court allows Sonam Wangchuk to shift to Medanta Hospital

title: Anantapur set to become garbage-free with new waste processing plant
content: Inspecting the municipal dumping yard on Gooty road, SACL Chairman and Anantapur Urban MLA say Swachh Andhra Corporation is set to establish a plant capable of processing 170 metric tonnes of waste daily at a cost of ₹17.50 crore

title: Panchayats propose single-window forest clearances, insurance cover and AI-based alerts to mitigate human-wildlife conflicts
content: Highlighting the developmental challenges faced by settlements in and around forests, local body representatives urge government to expedite approvals for essential infrastructure such as roads, drinking water projects and electricity connections

title: Kumkis to be deployed to reduce elephant-human conflict in Manyam
content: Aim is to provide immediate relief to the residents of villages along the Odisha border, where herds of wild elephants frequently enter Andhra Pradesh, damaging crops and property, and posing a threat to local communities

title: A.P. unveils White Paper on irrigation; ₹35,555-crore plan to complete 36 projects by 2028
content: The government has spent ₹24,611 crore on irrigation over the past two years, including ₹12,484 crore on projects in Rayalaseema, says Minister

title: Student of Shaheen science college scores 653 in NEET; college expects 448 MBBS seats
content: Students of the Shaheen Undergraduate Science College, Bidar, have made remarkable performances in the National Eligibility-cum-Entrance Test (NEET), with Muhammad Sajid Akbar emerging as the institution’s top scorer with 653 marks

title: Parliament fails to function for the second day
content: Opposition members protest over alleged NEET paper leak, police crackdown on students protesting in national capital and Ram Temple donation theft

title: LSGD funds shortage may hit palliative care delivery at local level
content: Palliative care nurses posted for community palliative care in many grama panchayats, especially in northern districts, have not received their salaries for months. Many LSGIs are also unable to pay the salary of ambulance drivers, meet fuel expenses, buy the necessary consumables — diapers, catheters, surgical gloves or medicines

title: Kerala University Syndicate explores PSC recruitment for aided college teachers
content: The Syndicate entrusted its sub-committee on aided college teaching and non-teaching staff to study the proposal and submit its recommendations

title: Mysuru: Student groups decry police crackdown on ‘Sansad Chalo’ protesters
content: AIDSO criticises the police for denying permission to the march despite it having been announced well in advance, and said the right to protest is a fundamental democratic right.

title: CPI(M) to step up agitation seeking AIIMS at Kinalur in Kozhikode
content: Party MLA P.A. Mohamed Riyas says he will meet the Chief Minister and the Health Minister to discuss the issue

title: A.P. signs MoU with UAE to set up food cluster; ₹100-crore processing unit proposed in Chittoor
content: Dubai-based Floor Masters International has plans to use A.P. as its export base for 25 countries; UAE Minister of Economy and Tourism lauds government’s investor-friendly policy

title: Walk the talk on protecting tribal rights, CPI(M) tells CM Naidu
content: The State government should implead in the case pending in Supreme Court on 1/70 regulation and issue an order replacing G.O. 3, says CPI(M) leader Srinivasa Rao

title: TUDA proposes new road to connect major national highways near Tirupati
content: It proposes a new 100-feet road connecting Tirupati-Chittoor-Bengaluru national highway 140 and Tirupati-Anantapur national highway 71, passing through Ithepalli, A. Rangampeta and Kalyani Dam

title: ‘This is gundagardi’, Uttarakhand High Court pulls up State over activist’s detention ahead of CJP protest
content: A Division Bench of Justices Ravindra Maithani and Siddhartha Sah questioned the legal basis for preventing Uttarakhand Parivartan Party chief Prabhat Dhyani from travelling to Delhi to join the protest

title: Rahul, Priyanka, Akhilesh detained, forcibly removed from protest site near PM’s residence
content: As police swooped down on the site, the leader of opposition in the Lok Sabha could be seen lying on the ground and resisting as security personnel tried to pick him up. He was hurt in the process

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar not concerned about drought situation in Karnataka, alleges BJP State president B.Y. Vijayendra
content: Except for a video conference with Deputy Commissioners, the Chief Minister has done nothing to handle the drought situation, Mr. Vijayendra claims

title: Four held in Kilimanoor abduction case
content: The gang purportedly executed the crime after the complainants refused a marriage proposal by the prime accused, Sudheesh

title: Farmers breach Baldota factory premises during protest in Koppal
content: A protest organised as part of the 46th Farmers’ Martyrs’ Day turned tense near the Baldota factory on the Koppal-Bengaluru National Highway on Tuesday after hundreds of farmers breached the factory premises by breaking sections of the compound wall and damaging the main gate

title: Kerala earmarks ₹15 crore for road safety under ₹450-crore Sabarimala development plan
content: The package envisaged comprehensive infrastructure development of the pilgrimage corridor, says Public Works Minister P.K. Basheer.

title: BJP trying to get pro-Congress voters’ names deleted, alleges AICC secretary Anjali Nimbalkar
content: AICC secretary and SIR in charge for Kittur Karnataka region Anjali Nimbalkar has alleged that BJP was trying to get the names of pro Congress voters deleted from the electoral rolls through the Special Intensive Revision

title: Supreme Court suggests Sonam Raghuvanshi surrender in Meghalaya honeymoon murder case
content: Bench hearing Meghalaya government’s appeal against bail asks murder accused to consider voluntary surrender, says it may revisit her bail plea after key prosecution witnesses are examined

title: Monsoon deficit threatens Onam crops in Kerala
content: Season-specific floriculture, which expanded significantly across regional clusters to cater to the festive demand for Pookkalams has been hit hard. Flower crops such as marigold and jasmine are suffering from moisture stress, leading to stunted plant development and premature drying

title: Chemo treatment for Bengal tigress at Thiruvananthapuram Zoo after being diagnosed with cancer
content: Babita was diagnosed with adenocarcinoma, which affects the mesothelial lining surrounding the internal organs, several months ago

title: CFTRI, NABARD join hands to bolster agro-food processing in Kalyana Karnataka
content: The collaboration aims to support the Farmer Training and Common Facility Centres established by NABARD across seven districts, namely Bidar, Yadgir, Kalaburagi, Raichur, Koppal, Ballari and Vijayanagara.

title: Sonam Wangchuk Shifted From Safdarjung Hospital To Medanta After Court Order
content: The court asked the director of Medanta Hospital to form a team of doctors with requisite expertise to constantly monitor Wangchuk.

title: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Congress Leaders Removed From Protest Site Outside PM's House
content: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi protested outside PM Modi's house. He and other leaders of his party were later detained.

title: Video: Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Detained From Protest Site, Taken Away In Bus
content: Congress workers, led by Rahul Gandhi, Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, marched to PM Modi's residence to protest the paper leak issue on Tuesday.

title: AAP's Swipe As Rahul Gandhi Stages Sit-In Protest Outside PM Modi's Home
content: Rahul Gandhi's protest outside the PM's house is an attempt to undermine the protests by the Cockroach Janta Party, the AAP claimed.

title: "Went Back On His Word": Minister Jitendra Singh On Rahul Gandhi's 'Demands'
content: Singh said the Centre agreed to the Congress' demand for discussions in Parliament, but another clause was added by Gandhi.

title: Shipowner Offers Sailors 6-Fold Pay To Cross Strait Of Hormuz: Report
content: According to a document seen by Bloomberg, a company offered seafarers an additional six months' salary if they completed a round trip through the strait to collect crude oil from Saudi Arabia or Iraq and unload it in the Gulf of Oman.

title: Adani Total Gas Q1 Revenue Rises 27% To Rs 1,910 Crore
content: The company said its operational performance remained strong during the quarter, with combined standalone CNG and PNG sales volumes rising 13 per cent year-on-year to 303 million metric standard cubic metres (MMSCM).

title: Man Accused Of Killing 6 Child Welfare Workers In Germany Found Dead In Jail
content: The suspect, who has been partially identified in German media as 45-year-old Fatih Khan G., was found dead by jail staff in his single cell early Tuesday.

title: 10 Arrested for Attacking Civic Officials During Bengaluru Eviction Drive
content: Violence erupted during an anti-footpath encroachment drive in Bengaluru's Shivajinagar after street vendors allegedly attacked Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) officials who had arrived to clear encroachments from public footpaths.

title: Soy Milk, Pasta And A Prison Plea: US Mercenary Wins Food Relief In Tihar Jail
content: Counsel for Matthew VanDyke argued at length before the court that his client had been unable to eat the prison diet because it was "spicy, oily, deep-fried and greasy", and that his medical condition required a different diet.

title: Army Rescues 571 People From Flood-Hit Areas In Assam
content: Dedicated Flood Relief Columns from the Spear Corps have been carrying out day-and-night rescue operation since on Jul 19, officials said.

title: Kuwait Says Iran Hit Several Power, Water Desalination Plants
content: Kuwait's ministry of electricity confirmed fires at several facilities due to repeated attacks by Iran over four days.

title: 20-Year-Old Woman In ICU After Being Injured At CJP Protests In Delhi
content: The woman, who remains on ventilator support, collided with a barricade during police crackdown on protesters at Jantar Mantar.

title: CJP Sacks Spokesperson Over Burger Video. "I'm Not Accountable," He Says
content: The CJP said in a statement that it "strongly condemns the deeply insensitive actions" of Vijeta Dahiya, whose videos surfaced on social media during Monday's march.

title: Ex-Cop, His Family Beat Farmer To Death Over Land Dispute In Rajasthan
content: The accused, a neighbouring retired ASI, wanted to possess the land and was pressuring the victim, Sushil to transfer it in his name.

title: 70 km To 500 Metres: Two New Ladakh Bridges To Boost Border Tourism
content: A new motorable bridge in Ladakh's Nubra Valley is expected to reduce a nearly 70-km journey to a crossing of about 500 metres, making remote border villages easier to reach for residents and tourists.

title: Explained: Why Ladakh Is Facing Extreme Wet And Dry Spells
content: 2024 was the only year in this period when rainfall was not in the large-excess category with Ladakh receiving 17 per cent more rain than normal.

title: 144 Refugees Dead Or Missing Since Mauritania Maritime Incidents: UN Report
content: The UN refugee agency UNHCR said that overall, three rescue and disembarkation operations were conducted between July 14 and 18 in Nouadhibou, Mauritania, bringing 387 people of various nationalities safely ashore.

title: Methane Gas Leak Triggered Sikkim Tunnel Collapse That Killed 10: Sources
content: At least ten people have been reported dead in the incident, while 12 have been rescued and over 18 are still feared trapped.

title: Char Dham Yatra Resumes In Uttarakhand After Temporary Suspension
content: According to the officials, the pilgrimage, suspended on Monday, has been smoothly restarted following a necessary review of the travel routes.

title: Middle East crisis, South China Sea tensions dominate Asean top diplomats meetings
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. title: CJP removes spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya, condemns 'deeply insensitive actions'
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. title: US student visa rules are changing on September 15: Who could be affected and how
content: the changes are aimed at curbing visa abuse, strengthening security checks and bringing greater federal oversight to international students . the new rules introduce a maximum four-year stay for most students, shorten post-study grace period and require government approval for extensions beyond the authorised stay . more than 3.6 lakh Indians were enrolled in American universities during the 2024-25 academic year .
